label: cli-launch-and-prompt-type
source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iqNzfK4_meQ ("Using OpenAI Codex CLI with GPT-5-Codex", official OpenAI YouTube channel) @ 00:41.5-00:52.5
interaction: Real macOS Terminal recording (screen-recorded insert within the vendor video, not a talking-head shot): types the command "codex" character-by-character at a bare zsh prompt, presses enter, and the Codex CLI TUI welcome screen renders immediately (banner, model/reasoning/directory info box, '/init /status /approvals /model' command list, empty composer with placeholder 'Write tasks for @filename', footer hints 'send / newline / transcript / quit'). The clip continues into the user typing a natural-language prompt ('Write a plan to implement multiplayer') into the composer character-by-character, showing real caret/typing idiom and cursor blink, ending just before submission.
note: Login/desktop-app caveats do not apply here: this is the CLI (terminal), not the undriveable Codex desktop app, and no auth was performed by the agent -- pure vendor video frame-mining, no live session touched. Video is 3840x2160@23.976fps; clip re-encoded with libx264 crf18. What this clip calibrates: (1) per-character typing cadence and caret behavior in the Codex CLI composer, (2) the near-instant (sub-frame at fps=2) appearance of the welcome/status TUI after the launch command commits -- there is no visible loading spinner or streamed boot text, it appears fully rendered on the very next sampled frame, (3) exact welcome-screen layout/copy for the composited 'codex-terminal' surface. This clip does NOT cover the agent's token-streamed response to a submitted prompt (the source video cuts to a talking-head shot immediately after the prompt is typed, before submission); a follow-up clip from the same source's ~00:170-181s range shows a 'codex resume' session picker and a resumed transcript scroll-in that partially covers post-submission rendering, and could be pulled later if the motion judge flags streamed-response pacing as still uncalibrated.
frameExtraction: ffmpeg -i clip.mp4 -vf fps=2 frames/f-%03d.png (22 frames over 11s)
appVersion: Codex CLI as shown in vendor video (unpinned; local machine's own `codex --version` at acquisition time was codex-cli 0.135.0, for cross-reference only -- not the version in the footage)
